summary: HIGH: normalizeCanonicalAuditSourceData strips pr_merge.canonical_audit but not pr_merge.roi_ledger_promotion, which merge-manager.js attaches between the two persistence passes via the identical bookkeeping mechanism. On the compact evidence path this leaks into raw_artifact_manifest digests, defeating IAP-CONTRACT-002/003 dedupe and reproducing the duplicate-commit bug for merges that cross the compact budget (reviewer reproduced empirically). MEDIUM: withCanonicalAuditBookkeeping test helper simulates only canonical_audit injection, never roi_ledger_promotion, so 9/9 focused + 3/3 CLI + 860/860 full suite stay silently green.

inspection.summary: Traced merge-manager.js double-promotion sequence against the fix's field stripping, ran focused tests (9/9), wrote standalone repro scripts against src/canonical-audit.js on both the default path (no leak) and compact path (leak reproduced: promoted_at and 3 files differ across passes).
inspection.evidence: standalone Node repro against promoteCanonicalAuditArtifacts with overBudget verification-evidence fixture (digest/promoted_at mismatch reproduced); node --test focused 9/9

judgment_delta: initial: diff looked complete against its own spec with all tests green -> final: needs_changes, because tracing the exact merge-manager call sequence revealed roi_ledger_promotion is injected identically to canonical_audit but not excluded, empirically reproducing the duplicate-commit mismatch on the compact path
